zh.lifeSummary: 當Sir Robert FitzWalter 1st Lord FitzWalter於1247年出生在英國艾可武德哈姆 沃爾特時，他的父親Walter FitzRobert Baron of Little Dunmow29歲，母親Ida Longespée The Younger26歲。他於大約1274年與Devorguille De Burgh結婚。他們至少是1個兒子和3個女兒的父母。他去世於1326年1月18日在英國艾可蕾托 登莫福享年79歲，埋葬在英國英格蘭艾可蕾托 登莫福。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Sir Robert FitzWalter 1st Lord FitzWalter于1247年出生在英国艾可武德哈姆 沃爾特时，他的父亲Walter FitzRobert Baron of Little Dunmow29岁，母亲Ida Longespée The Younger26岁。他于大约1274年与Devorguille De Burgh结婚。他们至少是1个儿子和3个女儿的父母。他去世于1326年1月18日在英国艾可蕾托 登莫福享年79岁，埋葬在英国英格兰艾可蕾托 登莫福。

nameMeanings.name: FitzWalter
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of Family Names in Britain and Ireland © University of the West of England 2016
meanings: Norman, English, Irish: relationship name from Anglo-Norman French fiz ‘son’ ( see Fitz ) + the personal name Walter , ‘son of Walter’.

variants.name: Fitzwater
meanings: Irish: altered form of Fitzwalter, a patronymic from Anglo-Norman French fi(t)z ‘son’ (see Fitz ) + the personal name Walter , meaning ‘son of Walter’.

History: This is the name of a family of Norman origin, descended from Godfrey, Count of Brionne (died c. 1010). Godfrey's grandson, Richard FitzGilbert, accompanied William the Conqueror and was made Lord of Clare. His great-grandson was Robert FitzWalter, one of the barons who guaranteed the Magna Carta in 1215.

variants.name: Walter
meanings: English (of Norman origin), German, Dutch, Polish, Swedish, and Danish: from a personal name composed of the ancient Germanic elements wald ‘rule’ + heri, hari ‘army’. This personal name was introduced into England both in the reign of Edward the Confessor and by the Normans (in the forms Walt(i)er, Waut(i)er). After the Norman Conquest it soon became one of the most popular personal names in Britain. Compare Walther .
Germanized or Americanized form of Czech, Slovak, Croatian, and Slovenian Valter and Czech Valtr: from the personal name Valter, Valtr, of ancient Germanic origin (see 1 above).

variants.name: Fitz
meanings: English: from Anglo-Norman French fi(t)z ‘son’ (from Latin filius), used originally to distinguish a son from a father bearing the same personal name and then conventionalized as a hereditary surname, or short for a surname of which fiz ‘son’ was the first element, for example Fitzhenry and Fitzroy .
German: from a vernacular pet form of the personal name Vinzenz (see Vincent ) or Vitus (see Vito , compare Veit ). Compare also Fietz .

nameMeanings.name: Robert
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: One of the many French names of Germanic origin that were introduced into Britain by the Normans; it has since remained in continuous use. It is derived from the nearly synonymous elements hrōd ‘fame’ + berht ‘bright, famous’, and had a native Old English predecessor of similar form (Hreodbeorht), which was supplanted by the Norman name. Two dukes of Normandy in the 11th century bore the name: the father of William the Conqueror (sometimes identified with the legendary Robert the Devil), and his eldest son. It was borne also by three kings of Scotland, notably Robert the Bruce ( 1274–1329 ), who freed Scotland from English domination. The altered short form Bob is very common, but Hob and Dob, which were common in the Middle Ages and gave rise to surnames, are extinct. See also Rupert .

historicEvents.title: 100 Years of Conflict Between England and France Ends
historicEvents.description: Treaty of Paris was signed on December 4, 1259, which ended 100 years of conflict between England and France.

historicEvents.title: Jews Expelled From England
historicEvents.description: After years of anti-Semitic behavior, King Edward I expelled Jews from England in 1290.

historicEvents.title: Famine Strikes Europe
historicEvents.description: A famine caused by cold winters and wet summers across Europe caused millions to die due to starvation. Desperation from hunger caused high levels of crime, stampedes in bread lines in London, infanticide, and even cannibalism throughout Europe.

en.lifeSummary: When Sir Robert FitzWalter 1st Lord FitzWalter was born in 1247, in Woodham Walter, Essex, England, his father, Walter FitzRobert Baron of Little Dunmow, was 29 and his mother, Ida Longespée The Younger, was 26. He married Devorguille De Burgh about 1274. They were the parents of at least 1 son and 3 daughters. He died on 18 January 1326, in Little Dunmow, Essex, England, at the age of 79, and was buried in Little Dunmow, Essex, England, United Kingdom.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Sir Robert FitzWalter 1st Lord FitzWalter est né en 1247, à Woodham Walter, Essex, Angleterre, son père, Walter FitzRobert Baron of Little Dunmow, avait 29 ans et sa mère, Ida Longespée The Younger, avait 26 ans. Il a épousé Devorguille De Burgh environ 1274. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils et 3 filles. Il est décédé le 18 janvier 1326, à Little Dunmow, Essex, Angleterre, à l’âge de 79 ans, et a été enterré à Little Dunmow, Essex, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni.

de.lifeSummary: Als Sir Robert FitzWalter 1st Lord FitzWalter 1247 in Woodham Walter, Essex, England geboren wurde, war sein Vater Walter FitzRobert Baron of Little Dunmow 29 und seine Mutter Ida Longespée The Younger 26. Er hat um 1274 Devorguille De Burgh geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Sohn und 3 Töchter. Er starb am 18. Januar 1326 im Alter von 79 Jahren in Little Dunmow, Essex, England und wurde in Little Dunmow, Essex, England, United Kingdom begraben.

ja.lifeSummary: Sir Robert FitzWalter 1st Lord FitzWalterが1247年にイギリスエセックスウッドハム ワルターで生まれた時，父Walter FitzRobert Baron of Little Dunmowは29歳，母Ida Longespée The Youngerは26歳であった。約1274年にDevorguille De Burghと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の息子と3人の娘の親であった。彼は1326年1月18日にイギリスエセックスリットル ダンモウで79歳で死去し，イギリスイギリスエセックスリットル ダンモウにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Sir Robert FitzWalter 1st Lord FitzWalter(이)가 1247년 영국 에세스 우드함 월터에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Walter FitzRobert Baron of Little Dunmow(은)는 29세였고, 그의 어머니 Ida Longespée The Younger(은)는 26세였다. 그는 대략 1274년 Devorguille De Burgh(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들과 세 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1326년 1월 18일 영국 에세스 리틀 던모에서 79세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 잉글랜드 에세스 리틀 던모에 묻혔다.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Sir Robert FitzWalter 1st Lord FitzWalter родился в 1247 г. в местности вудхам валтир, эссекс, Англия, его отцу по имени Walter FitzRobert Baron of Little Dunmow было 29 лет, а матери, которую звали Ida Longespée The Younger, 26. Он женился на женщине по имени Devorguille De Burgh около 1274 г. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын и 3 дочери. Он умер 18 января 1326 г. в местности литл дунмоу, эссекс, Англия в возрасте 79 лет и был похоронен в местности литл дунмоу, эссекс, Англия, Великобритания.
